perturbation.original_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name, fame, nickname, autograph, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.changed_text: A. CONSULTANT'S "ENDORSEMENT" means the right to use the CONSULTANT'S name and autograph in connection with the marketing, advertising, promotion and sale of ADAMS GOLF'S PRODUCT.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'fame, nickname, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image', from the definition of 'ENDORSEMENT', while other sections of the contract still refer to the broader definition of endorsement encompassing these elements (specifically sections related to advertising approvals and promotional appearances using likeness or voice), this creates an in-text contradiction. It creates ambiguity around whether ADAMS GOLF can use Tom Watson's likeness in advertising materials.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prior to publishing or placing any advertising or promotional material which uses CONSULTANT'S name, facsimile signature, nickname, voice or likeness, ADAMS GOLF shall submit the same to CONSULTANT, or CONSULTANT'S designee, for approval, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. If CONSULTANT disapproves, the reasons therefore shall be given to ADAMS GOLF in writing within three (3) business days or shall be deemed approved. ADAMS GOLF agrees to protect, indemnify and hold CONSULTANT harmless from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, arising out of, or in any way connected with, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, the company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "ENDORSEMENT" by removing 'fame, nickname, voice, facsimile, signature, photograph, likeness, and image.' The contradicted text refers to 'CONSULTANT'S name, facsimile signature, nickname, voice or likeness'. Because the definition of endorsement in the changed text no longer explicitly includes "voice or likeness", it creates a contradiction regarding whether Adams Golf needs approval from the consultant before using these elements in advertising materials, as specified in the contradicted text. The omission creates ambiguity and thus a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text:
5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF PRODUCT
During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. (It is expressly understood by the parties that CONSULTANT may play [* ****] clubs in the bag other than ADAMS GOLF clubs including, but not limited to, a putter by a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF but may not endorse those clubs and/or putter.)
perturbation.changed_text:
5. EXCLUSIVE USE OF PRODUCT
During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause about using a putter from another manufacturer creates a contradiction. Section 5 now states exclusively using MANDATORY PRODUCTS. This removal contradicts Section 6 which assumes and regulates CONSULTANT'S endorsement of non-competitive products, implying that he may use such products under certain conditions, especially when it comes to putters.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If CONSULTANT endorses or promotes a non-competitive product and in that endorsement or promotion CONSULTANT wears, plays, uses, holds or is in any way associated with a product that would constitute PRODUCT as defined under this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall use objectively reasonable best efforts to ensure that PRODUCT is an ADAMS GOLF PRODUCT and it shall not be altered or changed in appearance in the endorsement in any manner whatsoever without the express written consent of ADAMS GOLF. When endorsing a non-competitive product, under no circumstances shall CONSULTANT wear, play, use, hold or in any way be associated with an ADAMS GOLF competitor's Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the CONSULTANT to use a putter from a manufacturer other than ADAMS GOLF. The changed text removes this allowance, stating that the CONSULTANT shall exclusively play/use the MANDATORY PRODUCT. This contradicts the contradicted text, which discusses the CONSULTANT'S endorsement of non-competitive products, implying that he may use such products under certain conditions. Specifically, the removal conflicts with the scenario where the CONSULTANT endorses a non-competitive product (like a putter) and is associated with a product that would constitute PRODUCT as defined under this agreement, as mentioned in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text:
18. BASE COMPENSATION
For the entire term of this Agreement, from September 1, 2004 through [*****] ADAMS GOLF shall pay CONSULTANT a base compensation of [*****] dollars. The base compensation shall be paid [*****].
perturbation.changed_text:
18. BASE COMPENSATION
For the entire term of this Agreement, from September 1, 2004 through [*****] ADAMS GOLF shall pay CONSULTANT a base compensation of [*****] dollars.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the payment schedule for the base compensation, creates ambiguity. Section 18 now doesn't specifies when the compensation will be given. Because the payment schedule of base compensation, have been ommited, this will affect termination clause, which calculates the repayment amount based on that information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any other paragraph of this Agreement, in the event that CONSULTANT dies or is unable to play tour golf at all, ADAMS GOLF may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days' written notice. Upon such termination, CONSULTANT and/or CONSULTANT'S estate shall be required to repay ADAMS GOLF for that period of the Agreement not performed by CONSULTANT. The repayment amount shall be calculated in the same way as repayment is calculated under paragraph 8A.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the payment schedule for the base compensation, creating ambiguity regarding when the compensation will be paid. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which refers to paragraph 8A for calculating the repayment amount upon termination of the agreement due to CONSULTANT's death or inability to play golf. Without a defined payment schedule (originally in paragraph 18 but now removed), the calculation method referenced in the 'Contradicted Text' becomes impossible to apply, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty regarding the repayment obligations.
